channel.title: flove making-of — navigation questions
channel.description: New navigation design questions from flove interviews.

item.title: Should docs have cross-links between pages?
item.description:
• Inline links in text — Natural hyperlinks within content. Best UX, organic discovery. • Related-docs section — Explicit 'Related' block at the bottom of each page. Structured but rigid. • Both — Inline + related section. Maximum discoverability, maximum maintenance. • None — No cross-links — each page is standalone. Simplest.
★ decided: — · ◇ recommended: Inline links in text

item.title: What navigation pattern for docs?
item.description:
• Sidebar — Persistent left sidebar with section links. Standard docs pattern, always visible. • Top nav bar — Horizontal nav at the top. Cleaner, less space, but limited depth. • Breadcrumbs — Path-based breadcrumbs at the top. Good for deep hierarchies, less for flat ones. • None needed — No dedicated nav — just links in content. Simplest.
★ decided: — · ◇ recommended: Sidebar
